body:
## Summary

`scripts/format-release-notes.mjs` only strips a single leading `v` before interpolating the release version into a shields.io `/badge/<label>-<message>-<color>` URL. When the caller passes a language-prefixed tag such as `js-v1.7.12` or `rust-v0.3.4`, the resulting URL contains extra unescaped `-` separators and shields.io returns the SVG `404: badge not found`.

This template works correctly for consumers that use a plain `v<version>` tag, but any downstream repo that multiplexes JS and Rust releases under `js-v<version>` / `rust-v<version>` tags hits the bug. For concrete evidence see [link-assistant/web-capture#98](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/issues/98) and the [fix PR link-assistant/web-capture#99](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/pull/99).

## Reproduction

```bash
curl -sS 'https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-js-v1.7.12-blue.svg' | head -c 200
```

Returns an SVG whose `aria-label="404: badge not found"`. The working form is:

```bash
curl -sS 'https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-1.7.12-blue.svg' | head -c 200
```

Which returns `aria-label="npm: 1.7.12"`.

## Offending line

`scripts/format-release-notes.mjs`:

```js
const versionWithoutV = version.replace(/^v/, '');
```

`version.replace(/^v/, '')` strips a leading single `v`. Given `js-v1.7.12`, `versionWithoutV` stays `js-v1.7.12`, and the next line interpolates it into a URL with four `-`-separated fields instead of the three that shields.io parses.

## Suggested fix

Pick one of the following. All are backward-compatible with consumers that pass a plain `v<version>`:

1. **Normalize the passed string** (what link-assistant/web-capture#99 ships):

   ```js
   const versionWithoutV = version
     .replace(/^[a-z]+-/i, '')   // drop "js-", "rust-", etc.
     .replace(/^v/i, '');        // drop leading "v"
   ```

2. **Use the dynamic shields endpoint** so the badge reads the current npm version directly and does not depend on the tag string:

   ```js
   const npmBadge = `[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/${PACKAGE_NAME}?label=npm&color=blue)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/${PACKAGE_NAME})`;
   ```

3. **Encourage callers to pass the clean numeric version** from `steps.publish.outputs.published_version` rather than the tag. `scripts/format-github-release.mjs` currently passes the full tag - it could strip the prefix at the call site instead.

## Workaround for affected downstreams

Until upstream fixes this, downstream consumers can override the version normalization as in link-assistant/web-capture@7ee8fe6 and optionally run a one-shot repair like [link-assistant/web-capture's fix-existing-release-badges.mjs](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/blob/main/scripts/fix-existing-release-badges.mjs) against already-published releases.

## Related

- Rust pipeline template sidesteps the issue because it uses `/crates/v/<name>` (dynamic) rather than `/badge/...` (static).
- shields.io /badge syntax reference: https://shields.io/badges/static-badge

comments.body:
Use all the best practices from CI/CD templates (check full file tree to compare for all GitHub workflow and CI/CD scripts file), if the same issue is found in template report issue also in templates:
- https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template

We should compare all files, so we don't have more CI/CD errors in the future and reuse all the best practices from these templates.

We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, do as much as possible in one go, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

title: Shields badge URL breaks when release-version contains "js-v" / "rust-v" prefix
